text: Which of these moments hits closest to home right now?

options.text: Scrolling the news and feeling that low-grade dread that never quite goes away anymore
Watching videos of people living abroad and thinking "that could be me" — then closing the tab
Running the numbers on what your life could look like somewhere with a lower cost of living
Dreaming about a morning where you wake up and just... breathe. No performance. No armor.
Feeling this quiet fury — like you're done shrinking yourself for a country that doesn't deserve you

text: Be honest — what does your ideal morning look like?
sub: Don't overthink it. Your gut answer is the right one.

options.text: Coffee on a terrace, warm light, no agenda — just watching the neighborhood wake up
A trail run or swim somewhere beautiful before most people are even awake
Deep work session in a great café, then lunch with people who actually challenge me
Community — a farmers market, a conversation, feeling like I belong to something
Reading the news without my stomach dropping. Just... a morning that doesn't cost me something emotionally

text: Which of these descriptions makes you feel the most "that's me"?
sub: Pick the one that lands, even if it's not perfect.

options.text: I want to feel safe in my body, in my neighborhood, in my doctor's office — all at once
I want to be somewhere so beautiful it rewires how I think about what's possible
I want to live somewhere that actually reflects my values — not just tolerates me
I want to build something — a business, a life, a future — with real infrastructure under it
I want to feel settled and rooted in a way I honestly haven't felt in decades

text: When you imagine waking up somewhere that genuinely fits you — what's the first thing you notice?
sub: Not where you are now. Where you'd actually want to be.

options.text: The quiet. No background hum of anxiety. Just actual quiet.
That I'm not scanning my environment before I reach for my partner's hand
The light. The landscape. Something that makes me feel alive just by looking at it
That my energy is going INTO something — building, creating — not into defending my right to exist
People living their lives as if everything is wonderful. And meaning it.

text: How would you honestly describe your current relationship with the country you're living in?
sub: No judgment either way. This helps us understand where you're starting from.

options.text: Exhausted. I've been fighting to belong here for so long I've forgotten what it feels like not to fight.
Disconnected. Like I'm watching my life happen from behind glass.
Angry. I built a good life here and now I feel like it's being taken apart piece by piece.
Curious. I'm not in crisis — I'm just starting to wonder if there's more.
Quietly planning. I've been thinking about this longer than most people know.

text: Perhaps you've already noticed — there are certain things you stopped doing because it felt like too much work to be yourself. Which resonates?
sub: The small costs. The ones that add up.

options.text: Editing what I say before I say it. Constantly. In almost every room.
Not displaying affection in public the way straight couples do without thinking twice
Shrinking my actual personality to make other people comfortable
Skipping events, places, or opportunities because the math on safety didn't work out
Honestly? I've stopped noticing. Which might be the most exhausting answer of all.

text: You might find yourself wondering — what would it feel like to live somewhere that didn't require all that from you? When that thought shows up, what does it look like?
sub: Just follow the thought wherever it goes.

options.text: Relief. Like putting down something heavy I didn't realize I'd been carrying.
Excitement — I can feel the version of myself who exists there. I just haven't met them yet.
Grief, actually. That I've spent this much of my life managing this instead of living it.
Clarity. Like I'd finally have the bandwidth to build what I actually came here to build.
Honestly — I can't quite imagine it yet. That's part of why I'm taking this quiz.

text: What's the thing you're most hoping a different life could give you — that this one hasn't?
sub: Go ahead and want what you want. This is a safe place to say it.

options.text: To feel settled. Rooted. Like I actually belong to the place I'm living in.
To be surrounded by beauty — landscapes, culture, food, people — that makes me feel alive
To live somewhere that treats my rights as obvious — not as something to be debated
To build real financial and creative freedom with the systems to support it
To feel safe. Genuinely, physically, medically safe — and to stop spending energy managing that.

text: Let's say you've been living abroad for six months. You're on a video call with a friend back home. They ask how you're doing. What do you say?
sub: Don't think about logistics. Just feel into the answer.

options.text: "I can't imagine going back. I really can't."
"I feel really settled and rooted here — something I haven't felt in decades."
"I'm not here to fit in — I'm here to shine. And for the first time, the place agrees."
"The business is actually working. The infrastructure here is real. I'm building."
"I've never felt more at home in my own body. That's the only way I know how to say it."

text: One last thing. When you picture yourself truly free, what does "free" actually mean to you?
sub: Your answer shapes everything we're about to show you.

options.text: Free means safe. Free means my body, my love, my healthcare — protected. Without having to fight for it.
Free means alive. Somewhere so beautiful and full of possibility that I feel it in my chest every day.
Free means aligned. Living somewhere whose laws and culture actually reflect what I believe about human dignity.
Free means built. A life I designed, with real systems under it, that can't be taken from me.
Free means still. A slow, deep life — a morning that's mine, a neighborhood that knows me, roots.
